stem: Let \( g(x) = [[a]]x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) \). Starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \), perform one step of Newton's method, \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{g(x_0)}{g'(x_0)} \). What is \( x_1 \)?
solution: Newton's method uses \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{g(x_0)}{g'(x_0)} \), where \( g'(x) = 2([[a]])x + ([[b]]) \). Substituting \( x_0 = [[x0]] \) gives \( x_1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( g(x_0) \) itself instead of dividing by \( g'(x_0) \) first.
This adds the correction term instead of subtracting it (a sign error in Newton's formula).
This drops the leading \( x_0 \) term and reports only \( g(x_0)/g'(x_0) \).
This uses the wrong derivative \( a x + b \), forgetting the factor of 2 on the \( x^{2} \) term.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.

stem: A differentiable function \( f \) satisfies \( f([[x0]]) = [[fx0]] \) and \( f'([[x0]]) = [[fpx0]] \). Using one step of Newton's method, \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{f(x_0)}{f'(x_0)} \), what is \( x_1 \)?
distractor_rationales: This adds the correction term instead of subtracting it (a sign error).
This drops the leading \( x_0 \) term.
This inverts the fraction \( f(x_0)/f'(x_0) \).
This multiplies by \( f'(x_0)/f(x_0) \) instead of subtracting \( f(x_0)/f'(x_0) \).
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.

stem: To approximate \( \sqrt{[[N]]} \), Newton's method is applied to \( f(x) = x^{2} - [[N]] \) starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \). What is the next iterate \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{f(x_0)}{f'(x_0)} \)?
distractor_rationales: This has a sign error inside the numerator, using \( x_0^{2}-N \) instead of \( x_0^{2}+N \).
This uses the arithmetic mean of \( x_0 \) and \( N \) instead of Newton's formula.
This forgets to divide by \( f'(x_0)=2x_0 \).
This drops the \( x_0^{2} \) term from the numerator.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.

stem: To approximate \( \sqrt[3]{[[N]]} \), Newton's method is applied to \( f(x) = x^{3} - [[N]] \) starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \). What is the next iterate \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{f(x_0)}{f'(x_0)} \)?
distractor_rationales: This omits Newton's correction entirely and just reports \( f(x_0) \) subtracted from \( x_0 \) without dividing by \( f'(x_0) \).
This has a sign error, using \( 2x_0^{3}-N \) instead of \( 2x_0^{3}+N \).
This forgets to divide by \( f'(x_0) = 3x_0^{2} \).
This drops the \( 2x_0^{3} \) term from the numerator.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.

stem: Newton's method can compute \( \dfrac{1}{[[N]]} \) without performing division, by finding a root of \( f(x) = \dfrac{1}{x} - [[N]] \). Starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \), what is the next iterate \( x_1 = x_0 - \dfrac{f(x_0)}{f'(x_0)} \)?
distractor_rationales: This has the sign of the whole expression flipped.
This drops the \( x_0 \) factor multiplying \( (2-Nx_0) \).
This drops the leading factor of \( x_0 \) outside the parentheses.
This guesses the answer is close to \( 1/N \) scaled by \( x_0 \) rather than applying Newton's formula.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
This is off by one from the correct next iterate.
